questions.question: Briefly describe two scenarios where a Full Table Scan (Sequential Scan) would be a more efficient access method than an Index Scan.
expectedPoints: When the query needs to retrieve a large percentage (e.g., >15-20%) of the rows from the table.
When the table is very small, making the overhead of using an index unnecessary.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Nested Loops Join and Hash Join algorithms. Explain the scenarios in which a query optimizer would likely choose one over the other.
expectedPoints: Nested Loops: Concept (for each row in outer, scan inner); Best for small outer table / indexed inner table; Complexity O(n*m).
Hash Join: Concept (build hash table from smaller table, probe with larger); Best for large, non-indexed joins on equality; Complexity ~O(n+m) but requires memory.
Optimizer Choice: Chooses NL if one set is small or inner is indexed. Chooses Hash if sets are large, no index, and sufficient memory is available.
Contrast: NL doesn't require memory but can be slow. Hash is fast but memory-dependent.
